questions.q:
Rearrange the words to make a correct sentence:
not / morning / she / go / work / today
opts: She not go work today morning.
Today, she does not go to work in the morning.
She does not go to work this morning.
She not work today go morning.
questions.exp: The most natural and grammatically accurate construction is 'She does not go to work this morning'.

questions.q:
Rearrange the words to make a correct sentence:
anyone / seen / you / have / the / latest / film
opts: Have you anyone seen the latest film?
Have you seen the latest film, anyone?
Have you seen the latest film anyone?
Have you seen anyone the latest film?
questions.exp: Addressing a group directly requires separating 'anyone' with a comma at the end.

questions.q:
Rearrange the words to make a correct sentence:
She / interested / that / was / in / proposal / said / she / the
opts: She was interested in that proposal said the she.
She said she was interested in that the proposal.
She said that she was proposal the interested in.
She said that she was interested in the proposal.
questions.exp: In reported speech, the correct syntax is 'She said that she was interested in the proposal'.

questions.q:
Rearrange the words to make a correct sentence:
great / in / 21st / the / shown / improvements / technology / century
opts: In the 21st century technology has shown great improvements.
The technology has shown improvements great in 21st century.
21st technology shown the century has improvements great in.
The technology 21st century has shown improvements great in.
questions.exp: The correct order starts with the time prepositional phrase followed by the subject and present perfect verb.

questions.q:
Rearrange the words to make a correct sentence:
Brother / with / my / distinction / has / University / from / graduated / Mettu
opts: My Mettu University has graduated with distinction from a brother.
My brother has graduated from Mettu University with distinction.
My brother from Mettu University has graduated with distinction.
From Mettu University has graduated my brother with distinction.
questions.exp: The sentence flows: Subject ('My brother') + present perfect verb ('has graduated') + prepositional phrase.

questions.q:
Rearrange the words to make a correct sentence:
Beautiful / woman / young / in / Wollo / studied / University / has / the / medicine
opts: The young beautiful woman has studied in Wollo University medicine.
The woman beautiful has studied medicine in young Wollo University.
The beautiful young woman has studied in Wollo University medicine.
The beautiful young woman has studied medicine in Wollo University.
questions.exp: Adjective order is opinion ('beautiful') before age ('young'). The transitive verb 'studied' takes the direct object 'medicine'.

questions.q:
Rearrange the words to make a correct sentence:
she / is / person / a / driving / not / should / use / phone / a / while / he / or
opts: A person he should not use a phone or while she is driving.
A phone should not use while she or he is driving a person.
A person should not use a phone while driving he or she.
A person should not use a phone while he or she is driving.
questions.exp: The logical rule is 'A person should not use a phone' followed by the temporal clause 'while he or she is driving'.

questions.q:
Rearrange the words to make a correct sentence:
our / order / not / us / write / teacher / to / anything
opts: Our teacher ordered us to not write anything.
To not write anything our teacher ordered us.
Our teacher ordered us not to write anything.
Not to write anything our teacher ordered us.
questions.exp: Reported negative commands use the structure 'ordered + object + not + to-infinitive'.

questions.q:
Rearrange the words to make a correct sentence:
carefully / the / examined / scientist / machine / damaged / the / conclusion / before / giving / a
opts: The scientist examined carefully the damaged machine before giving a conclusion.
The scientist carefully examined the damaged machine before giving a conclusion.
Carefully the scientist examined the damaged machine before giving a conclusion.
The carefully scientist examined the damaged machine before giving a conclusion.
questions.exp: The adverb 'carefully' correctly precedes the action verb 'examined'.

questions.q:
Rearrange the words to make a correct sentence:
scarcely / finished / had / the / principal / his / speech / when / went / off / the / electricity
opts: Scarcely the principal had finished his speech when the electricity went off.
Had scarcely the principal finished his speech when the electricity went off.
The principal scarcely had finished his speech when the electricity went off.
Scarcely had the principal finished his speech when the electricity went off.
questions.exp: Starting with the negative adverb 'Scarcely' requires inversion of the subject and auxiliary verb 'had'.

questions.q:
Organize the sentences logically:
1. They give us much information about history.
2. He wrote about the everyday life of the common people.
3. Shakespeare wrote 37 plays, dramas, tragedies and comedies.
4. His plays help us to understand people.
5. For example: Hamlet, King Lear....
questions.exp: Start with Shakespeare's plays (3), provide examples (5), elaborate on content (2), explain their value (4), and historical significance (1).

questions.q:
Organize the sentences logically:
1. Diseases can also be caused by a lack of hygiene.
2. Other diseases that are caused by insects are yellow fever, bilharzias... etc.
3. For example, malaria is caused by a certain type of mosquito.
4. Many diseases are caused by insects.
questions.exp: Introduce insect-borne diseases (4), offer malaria as an example (3), expand with other insect-borne diseases (2), and transition to hygiene causes (1).

questions.q:
Organize the sentences logically:
1. Early humans imitated these natural bridges by chopping down tall trees and placing them across water.
2. It was built of many logs tied together with ropes.
3. The first bridges were simply trees that had accidentally fallen across streams.
4. The first genuine bridge was laid across the Euphrates river at Babylon about 700BC.
5. The bridge over the Euphrates River lasted for decades.
questions.exp: Start with natural fallen trees (3), human imitation (1), more complex logs (2), engineered bridges (4), and their durability (5).

questions.q:
Organize the sentences logically:
a. Salt in some countries has remained fixed.
b. Due to value service is reportedly shortened.
c. That left many things disorderly and nasty.
d. What is more, the organization seems to lack successful management.
e. However, it is decreasing at the same time as markets are in crash.
questions.exp: The paragraph develops: general fixed state (a), market crash changes (e), direct consequences (b), organizational issues (d), and final state (c).

questions.q:
Organize the sentences logically:
a. Such people do it more for pleasure than necessity.
b. Weekend is the best time for those who buy essential things for life.
c. Very few people can avoid shopping at least once a week.
d. For some people, however, shopping has become a daily routine.
e. Shopping is a necessary part of life.
questions.exp: Establish shopping's necessity (e), describe weekly habits (c), present the contrast of daily routines (d), explain motivations (a), and conclude with weekend shoppers (b).

questions.q:
Organize the sentences logically:
a. The continent with the highest life expectancy is Europe.
b. Hence, associates and theorists often lived to great ages.
c. Previously, women died in childbirth and young men in wars.
d. Now, childbirth is not risky and there is little war.
e. The average age for men is 77 and for women is 82.
questions.exp: Introduce Europe (a) and its averages (e), describe historical risks (c), explain modern safety improvements (d), and draw the final conclusion (b).

questions.q:
Organize the sentences logically to form a dialogue:
a. Very well, thanks. Let’s get down to business, shall we?
b. I’m fine thanks. How are you?
c. Hello again! How are you getting on
d. Yes, all right.
questions.exp: The dialogue opens with a greeting (c), response and question (b), proposal to work (a), and agreement (d).

questions.q:
Organize the sentences logically:
a. The gadget is a really special one.
b. It converts oral texts into written ones in seconds.
c. Technology provides mankind with amazing gifts.
d. Transcription is no more seen as an exhaustively boring task.
e. Just play a talk and print out is there on your desk in no time.
questions.exp: Open with general technology (c), transition to a specific gadget (a), define its function (b), describe ease of use (e), and conclude with its impact (d).

questions.q:
Organize the sentences logically:
1. eventually, their confidence increased and they performed better in exams.
2. Grade 12 students initially struggled with time management during mock tests.
3. Teachers introduced strict exam simulation sessions to improve their skills.
4. Continuous practical helped students understand how to answer quickly and accurately.
5. As a result, students began to develop effective exam strategies.
questions.exp: Struggle (2) leading to teachers' intervention (3), which yields strategy development (5), continuous practice (4), and finally exam success (1).

questions.q:
Organize the sentences logically:
1. The teacher praised the students for their teamwork.
2. They completed the project before the deadline.
3. Our class was assigned a group project.
4. Each group member contributed actively.
5. We started working immediately after the instruction.
questions.exp: The assignment (3) leads to starting work (5), details of individual contribution (4), completion (2), and praise (1).

questions.q:
Technology in Farming
Farming has evolved dramatically over the past century, thanks to advancements in technology. From the development of better machinery to the use of computer systems, modern technology has greatly increased efficiency in agriculture. Tractors, combine harvesters, and planting machines have all improved the speed and accuracy of planting, cultivating, and harvesting crops. As a result, farmers can now produce more food with less labor and fewer resources.

Another area where technology has made a huge impact is in the management of crops and livestock. With the use of drones, sensors, and satellite imaging, farmers can now monitor crop health, soil conditions, and livestock well-being from a distance. This has allowed for more precise farming, where farmers can apply fertilizers, pesticides, and water only where needed, reducing waste and environmental harm.

Genetic engineering and biotechnology have also played a major role in transforming agriculture. By modifying the genetic makeup of plants and animals, scientists have created crops that are more resistant to pests, diseases, and extreme weather conditions. Additionally, genetically modified organisms (GMOs) have been designed to grow faster and with higher yields, ensuring food security in the face of a growing global population.

However, the integration of technology in farming has not come without challenges. While technology can increase productivity, it often requires a significant initial investment, which may be difficult for small farmers to afford. Additionally, there are concerns about the long-term environmental impact of technologies such as GMOs and the potential loss of biodiversity. As farming continues to modernize, it will be important for farmers, governments, and scientists to work together to address these challenges while ensuring sustainable food production.
What role has technology played in farming over the past century?
opts: Made farming completely independent of machinery
questions.exp: The first paragraph of the text states: 'modern technology has greatly increased efficiency in agriculture...'

questions.q:
Education in Ethiopia
Education in Ethiopia has seen substantial progress over the past few decades, with significant improvements in access, infrastructure, and literacy rates. The government has made education a priority, aiming to increase enrollment at all levels, especially in rural areas. Primary education is free and compulsory for children and enrollment rates have grown rapidly in recent years. However, challenges remain, including overcrowded classrooms, a lack of educational resources, and disparities between urban and rural areas. Despite these obstacles, the government is committed to addressing these issues and improving the quality of education across the country.

Secondary education is also expanding, with more schools being built in both urban and rural areas. While the number of students enrolled in secondary education has increased, there are still limitations, such as insufficient teachers and materials. Higher education in Ethiopia has grown significantly in the past two decades, with the establishment of new universities and colleges. Ethiopian universities have seen an influx of students, with many pursuing degrees in fields like engineering, health sciences, and business. However, the quality of education in higher institutions remains a concern, with some universities facing challenges related to outdated curricula, underfunded facilities, and the need for more qualified professors.

Despite the government's efforts to improve the educational system, Ethiopia faces challenges that hinder the development of its education sector. Poverty, cultural factors, and geographical barriers continue to limit access to education, particularly for girls and children in remote areas. The government, alongside international organizations, is working to address these issues by providing scholarships, building more schools, and offering adult literacy programs. Education is seen as a key to Ethiopia’s development, and the government is taking steps to ensure that more children and young adults receive the education they need to contribute to the country's future.
What is the primary focus of the Ethiopian government in the education sector?
opts: To improve access only to secondary education
To reduce the number of students enrolled
To prioritize education and increase enrollment rates, especially in rural areas
questions.exp: Paragraph 1 states: 'The government has made education a priority, aiming to increase enrollment at all levels, especially in rural areas.'

What is a major challenge faced by secondary education in Ethiopia?
opts: A lack of teachers and educational materials
questions.exp: Paragraph 2 states: 'While the number of students enrolled in secondary education has increased, there are still limitations, such as insufficient teachers and materials.'

Poverty, cultural factors, and geographical barriers continue to limit access to education, particularly for girls and children in remote areas. The government, alongside international organizations, is working to address these issues by providing scholarships, building more schools, and offering adult literacy programs. Education is seen as a key to Ethiopia’s development, and the government is taking steps to ensure that more children and young adults receive the education they need to contribute to the country's future.
How has higher education in Ethiopia developed over the past two decades?
opts: The number of universities and colleges has significantly increased
Higher education has become less accessible to students
The government has shut down several universities
questions.exp: Paragraph 2 states: 'Higher education in Ethiopia has grown significantly in the past two decades, with the establishment of new universities and colleges.'

questions.q:
Education in Ethiopia
Education in Ethiopia has seen substantial progress over the past few decades, with significant improvements in access, infrastructure, and literacy rates. The government has made education a priority, aiming to increase enrollment at all levels, especially in rural areas. Primary education is free and compulsory for children and enrollment rates have grown rapidly in recent years. However, challenges remain, including overcrowded classrooms, a lack of educational resources, and disparities between urban and rural areas. Despite these obstacles, the government is committed to addressing these issues and improving the quality of education across the country.

Secondary education is also expanding, with more schools being built in both urban and rural areas. While the number of students enrolled in secondary education has increased, there are still limitations, such as insufficient teachers and materials. Higher education in Ethiopia has grown significantly in the past two decades, with the establishment of new universities and colleges. Ethiopian universities have seen an influx of students, with many pursuing degrees in fields like engineering, health sciences, and business. However, the quality of education in higher institutions remains a concern, with some universities facing challenges related to outdated curricula, underfunded facilities, and the need for more qualified professors.

Despite the government's efforts to improve the educational system, Ethiopia faces challenges that hinder the development of its education sector. Poverty, cultural factors, and geographical barriers continue to limit access to education, particularly for girls and children in remote areas. The government, alongside international organizations, is working to address these issues by providing scholarships, building more schools, and offering adult literacy programs. Education is seen as a key to Ethiopia’s development, and the government is taking steps to ensure that more children and young adults receive the education they need to contribute to the country's future.
What is one of the ongoing barriers to education in remote areas of Ethiopia?
opts: The high number of qualified teachers
questions.exp: Paragraph 3 states: 'Poverty, cultural factors, and geographical barriers continue to limit access to education, particularly for girls and children in remote areas.'

questions.q:
Education in Ethiopia
Education in Ethiopia has seen substantial progress over the past few decades, with significant improvements in access, infrastructure, and literacy rates. The government has made education a priority, aiming to increase enrollment at all levels, especially in rural areas. Primary education is free and compulsory for children and enrollment rates have grown rapidly in recent years. However, challenges remain, including overcrowded classrooms, a lack of educational resources, and disparities between urban and rural areas. Despite these obstacles, the government is committed to addressing these issues and improving the quality of education across the country.

Secondary education is also expanding, with more schools being built in both urban and rural areas. While the number of students enrolled in secondary education has increased, there are still limitations, such as insufficient teachers and materials. Higher education in Ethiopia has grown significantly in the past two decades, with the establishment of new universities and colleges. Ethiopian universities have seen an influx of students, with many pursuing degrees in fields like engineering, health sciences, and business. However, the quality of education in higher institutions remains a concern, with some universities facing challenges related to outdated curricula, underfunded facilities, and the need for more qualified professors.

Despite the government's efforts to improve the educational system, Ethiopia faces challenges that hinder the development of its education sector. Poverty, cultural factors, and geographical barriers continue to limit access to education, particularly for girls and children in remote areas. The government, alongside international organizations, is working to address these issues by providing scholarships, building more schools, and offering adult literacy programs. Education is seen as a key to Ethiopia’s development, and the government is taking steps to ensure that more children and young adults receive the education they need to contribute to the country's future.
In the sentence, 'The government has made education a priority, aiming to increase enrollment at all levels, especially in rural areas,' what does the word 'enrollment' refer to?
opts: The registration or joining of students in school
questions.exp: Enrollment refers to the process of officially registering or joining a school to study.

questions.q:
Education in Ethiopia
Education in Ethiopia has seen substantial progress over the past few decades, with significant improvements in access, infrastructure, and literacy rates. The government has made education a priority, aiming to increase enrollment at all levels, especially in rural areas. Primary education is free and compulsory for children and enrollment rates have grown rapidly in recent years. However, challenges remain, including overcrowded classrooms, a lack of educational resources, and disparities between urban and rural areas. Secondary education is also expanding, with more schools being built in both urban and rural areas. However, the quality of education in higher institutions remains a concern, with some universities facing challenges related to outdated curricula, underfunded facilities, and the need for more qualified professors.

Despite the government's efforts to improve the educational system, Ethiopia faces challenges that hinder the development of its education sector. Poverty, cultural factors, and geographical barriers continue to limit access to education, particularly for girls and children in remote areas. The government, alongside international organizations, is working to address these issues by providing scholarships, building more schools, and offering adult literacy programs. Education is seen as a key to Ethiopia’s development, and the government is taking steps to ensure that more children and young adults receive the education they need to contribute to the country's future.
What is the meaning of 'compulsory' in the sentence: 'Primary education is free and compulsory for children'?
questions.exp: Compulsory means mandatory, required by law or rule; something that must be done.

questions.q:
Education in Ethiopia
Education in Ethiopia has seen substantial progress over the past few decades, with significant improvements in access, infrastructure, and literacy rates. The government has made education a priority, aiming to increase enrollment at all levels, especially in rural areas. Primary education is free and compulsory for children and enrollment rates have grown rapidly in recent years. However, challenges remain, including overcrowded classrooms, a lack of educational resources, and disparities between urban and rural areas. Despite these obstacles, the government is committed to addressing these issues and improving the quality of education across the country.

Secondary education is also expanding, with more schools being built in both urban and rural areas. While the number of students enrolled in secondary education has increased, there are still limitations, such as insufficient teachers and materials. However, the quality of education in higher institutions remains a concern, with some universities facing challenges related to outdated curricula, underfunded facilities, and the need for more qualified professors.

Despite the government's efforts to improve the educational system, Ethiopia faces challenges that hinder the development of its education sector. Poverty, cultural factors, and geographical barriers continue to limit access to education, particularly for girls and children in remote areas. The government, alongside international organizations, is working to address these issues by providing scholarships, building more schools, and offering adult literacy programs. Education is seen as a key to Ethiopia’s development, and the government is taking steps to ensure that more children and young adults receive the education they need to contribute to the country's future.
What does 'influx' mean in the sentence: 'Ethiopian universities have seen an influx of students'?
opts: A large arrival or flow of students
A change in the educational system
A lack of interest from students
questions.exp: An influx is a large, sudden arrival or entry of people or things.

questions.q:
Education in Ethiopia
Education in Ethiopia has seen substantial progress over the past few decades, with significant improvements in access, infrastructure, and literacy rates. The government has made education a priority, aiming to increase enrollment at all levels, especially in rural areas. Primary education is free and compulsory for children and enrollment rates have grown rapidly in recent years. However, challenges remain, including overcrowded classrooms, a lack of educational resources, and disparities between urban and rural areas. Education is seen as a key to Ethiopia’s development, and the government is taking steps to ensure that more children and young adults receive the education they need to contribute to the country's future.
What is the meaning of 'barriers' in 'Poverty, cultural factors, and geographical barriers continue to limit access to education'?
questions.exp: Barriers are obstacles, limits, or blockages that prevent easy movement or progress.

questions.q:
Education in Ethiopia
Education in Ethiopia has seen substantial progress over the past few decades, with significant improvements in access, infrastructure, and literacy rates. The government has made education a priority, aiming to increase enrollment at all levels, especially in rural areas. Primary education is free and compulsory for children and enrollment rates have grown rapidly in recent years. However, challenges remain, including overcrowded classrooms, a lack of educational resources, and disparities between urban and rural areas. Despite these obstacles, the government is committed to addressing these issues and improving the quality of education across the country.

Secondary education is also expanding, with more schools being built in both urban and rural areas. While the number of students enrolled in secondary education has increased, there are still limitations, such as insufficient teachers and materials. However, the quality of education in higher institutions remains a concern, with some universities facing challenges related to outdated curricula, underfunded facilities, and the need for more qualified professors.

Despite the government's efforts to improve the educational system, Ethiopia faces challenges that hinder the development of its education sector. Poverty, cultural factors, and geographical barriers continue to limit access to education, particularly for girls and children in remote areas. The government, alongside international organizations, is working to address these issues by providing scholarships, building more schools, and offering adult literacy programs. Education is seen as a key to Ethiopia’s development, and the government is taking steps to ensure that more children and young adults receive the education they need to contribute to the country's future.
What does 'disparities' mean in the sentence 'There are disparities between urban and rural areas'?
questions.exp: Disparities refers to clear differences, inequalities, or imbalances between things.

questions.q:
Education in Ethiopia
Education in Ethiopia has seen substantial progress over the past few decades, with significant improvements in access, infrastructure, and literacy rates. The government has made education a priority, aiming to increase enrollment at all levels, especially in rural areas. Primary education is free and compulsory for children and enrollment rates have grown rapidly in recent years. However, challenges remain, including overcrowded classrooms, a lack of educational resources, and disparities between urban and rural areas. Despite these obstacles, the government is committed to addressing these issues and improving the quality of education across the country.

Secondary education is also expanding, with more schools being built in both urban and rural areas. While the number of students enrolled in secondary education has increased, there are still limitations, such as insufficient teachers and materials. Higher education in Ethiopia has grown significantly in the past two decades, with the establishment of new universities and colleges. Ethiopian universities have seen an influx of students, with many pursuing degrees in fields like engineering, health sciences, and business. However, the quality of education in higher institutions remains a concern, with some universities facing challenges related to outdated curricula, underfunded facilities, and the need for more qualified professors.

Despite the government's efforts to improve the educational system, Ethiopia faces challenges that hinder the development of its education sector. Poverty, cultural factors, and geographical barriers continue to limit access to education, particularly for girls and children in remote areas. The government, alongside international organizations, is working to address these issues by providing scholarships, building more schools, and offering adult literacy programs. Education is seen as a key to Ethiopia’s development, and the government is taking steps to ensure that more children and young adults receive the education they need to contribute to the country's future.
How is the government working with international organizations to address access challenges?
opts: By providing scholarships, building schools, and offering adult literacy programs
questions.exp: The final paragraph explicitly states: 'The government, alongside international organizations, is working to address these issues by providing scholarships, building more schools, and offering adult literacy programs.'

questions.q:
Choose the word that has the same meaning as the underlined word:
The children were delighted by the magician's tricks.
questions.exp: Delighted means extremely pleased or happy; 'thrilled' is the closest synonym.

questions.q:
Choose the word that has the same meaning as the underlined word:
She swiftly completed her homework before going out to play.
questions.exp: Swiftly means with great speed; 'quickly' is its exact synonym.

questions.q:
Choose the word that has the same meaning as the underlined word:
The movie was very captivating, and the audience loved it.
questions.exp: Captivating means capable of attracting and holding interest; 'interesting' or 'fascinating' are close synonyms.

questions.q:
Choose the word that has the same meaning as the underlined word:
He was exhausted after working all day in the garden.
questions.exp: Exhausted means extremely physically tired or drained; 'tired' is the correct match.

questions.q:
Choose the word that has the same meaning as the underlined word:
She stubbornly refused to listen to anyone's advice.
questions.exp: Stubbornly means showing a dogged determination not to change one's attitude; 'obstinately' is its exact synonym.

questions.q:
Choose the word that has the same meaning as the underlined word:
The lecture was tedious, causing many students to lose focus.
questions.exp: Tedious means boring, slow, and monotonous; 'dull' is the closest synonym.

questions.q:
Choose the word that has the same meaning as the underlined word:
The manager’s decision was considered prudent, ensuring the company avoided unnecessary risks.
questions.exp: Prudent means acting with or showing care and thought for the future; 'cautious' or 'wise' is the synonym.

questions.q:
Choose the word that has the same meaning as the underlined word:
Slavery had been prohibited in many parts of Africa by the 19th century.
questions.exp: Prohibited means formally forbidden by law or authority; 'banned' is the closest synonym.

questions.q:
Choose the word that has the same meaning as the underlined word:
Procrastination is one of the main barriers keeping you from succeeding.
questions.exp: Procrastination is the action of delaying or postponing something needlessly.

questions.q:
Choose the word that has the same meaning as the underlined word:
Internationally, 17 road fatalities per 100,000 population per annum are reported.
questions.exp: Fatalities refers to deaths caused by accidents, disasters, or violence; 'death rates' matches this context.

questions.q:
Choose the alternative that best completes the sentence:
The professor's explanation was so __________ that all the students understood the topic clearly.
questions.exp: Because the students understood the topic 'clearly', the explanation must have been 'simple'.

questions.q:
Choose the alternative that best completes the sentence:
The team celebrated their __________ after winning the championship game.
questions.exp: Winning a championship game is a major achievement or 'success', which warrants celebration.

questions.q:
Choose the alternative that best completes the sentence:
The research results were __________ in a scientific journal for others to review.
questions.exp: For others to review scientific research, the results must be 'published' in a journal.

questions.q:
Choose the alternative that best completes the sentence:
The teacher’s __________ comments helped the students improve their writing skills.
questions.exp: Comments that help students improve their skills are supportive or 'encouraging'.

questions.q:
Choose the alternative that best completes the sentence:
The manager gave __________ instructions to ensure the project was completed on time.
questions.exp: To ensure tasks are completed correctly and on time, instructions must be precise or 'clear'.

questions.q:
Choose the alternative that best completes the sentence:
It is a good idea to keep a time __________ where you write down how long you spend on each task.
questions.exp: A record of actions or performance over time is called a 'log'.

questions.q:
Choose the alternative that best completes the sentence:
He asked me to do a lot of things, but I __________ from doing them because they were illegal.
questions.exp: To choose not to do an action is to 'refrain' from it. The correct past tense is 'refrained'.

questions.q:
Choose the alternative that best completes the sentence:
The dentist told me there were __________ reasons to brush my teeth, but I can only think of one.
questions.exp: The contrast between many reasons ('numerous') and 'only one' makes 'numerous' the logical fit.

questions.q:
Choose the alternative that best completes the sentence:
The attorney suggested that the witness’s testimony was __________ because the witness was a known liar.
questions.exp: A known liar's testimony cannot be trusted, making it 'unreliable'.

questions.q:
Choose the alternative that best completes the sentence:
Unlike many noisy industrial towns, Addis Ababa is a pleasant place to live, its people are __________.
questions.exp: The adjective form 'friendly' is required here to describe the people of Addis Ababa.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
____________ living in a big house, they only use the ground floor.
questions.exp: 'Despite' is a preposition and is followed directly by a gerund (-ing form), showing contrast.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
_________ I was walking home, it began to rain.
questions.exp: 'While' is used to introduce a background action that was occurring when another event started.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
The traders _____________ many cars since last year.
questions.exp: 'Traders' is a plural subject. The time marker 'since last year' requires the present perfect tense ('have sold').

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
The students__________ are studying in the library are my best friends.
questions.exp: The relative pronoun 'who' is used as the subject of the relative clause to refer to people ('students').

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
The meeting hall ___________ at the moment in order to serve our participants as usual.
questions.exp: The time marker 'at the moment' requires the present continuous passive tense ('is being decorated' for the singular 'hall').

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
Ato Degu, _________ wife is a nurse, goes to work on foot.
questions.exp: The possessive relative pronoun 'whose' is used to show ownership ('Ato Degu's wife').

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
They were exhausted highly because they ___________ all day.
questions.exp: The past continuous or past perfect continuous tense ('had been driving') describes a continuous past action that caused a past state of exhaustion.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
___________ she studied hard, she failed the exam.
questions.exp: 'Even though' is a conjunction used to introduce a clause showing strong contrast.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
I don’t think your friend is a good person. I advise you _________ thinking about her.
questions.exp: The verb 'advise' is followed by an object and a to-infinitive: 'advise you to stop'.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
He gets a stomachache ______________ he eats acidic foods.
questions.exp: 'Whenever' is a temporal adverb meaning 'at whatever time' or 'every time'.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
I _____________ for the exam before I realized it was canceled.
questions.exp: The past perfect tense ('had studied') describes an action completed prior to another past action ('realized').

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
Oranges _____________ from Paraguay.
questions.exp: 'Oranges' is a plural subject. The simple present passive ('are imported') describes a general fact.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
There is no hope of ______________ funding for our new research.
questions.exp: The preposition 'of' must be followed by a gerund ('getting').

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
I wish I ____________ a bigger car. Mine is too small.
questions.exp: To express a present wish or regret about a current state, we use the simple past tense ('had').

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
He went outside _____________ he could get some fresh air.
questions.exp: 'So that' is a conjunction used to show purpose, followed by a clause containing a modal verb ('could').

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
Many countries are adopting renewable energy; __________, Germany has significantly increased solar production.
questions.exp: 'For example' is used to introduce a specific case that illustrates a general statement.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
It is difficult _______________ why she left her job so suddenly.
questions.exp: The dummy subject 'It is + adjective' structure is followed by a to-infinitive ('to know').

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
The flight was delayed due to the storm. ____________, many passengers complained.
questions.exp: 'As a result' is a transition phrase showing a direct cause-and-effect relationship.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
It was a disgusting weekend; I didn’t ____________ anything fun.
questions.exp: The sentence already contains a negative 'didn't', so a simple infinitive verb ('do') is needed to avoid a double negative.

questions.q:
Choose the word that best completes the sentence:
Did you hear those dogs ____________ most of the night?
questions.exp: Verbs of perception (like 'hear') can be followed by an object and a participle ('barking') to show an ongoing action.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Seni: How long have you been using the internet in our school?
Zufan: _______________________________________.
opts: I don’t know how to use it.
For unlimited time I don’t have a smart phone.
Since the beginning of the 1st semester.
Before I joined here I used to get the information.
questions.exp: The question 'How long' requires a duration or a starting point in time, which 'Since the beginning of the 1st semester' provides.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Siraj: ___________________________________?
Semir: If it is available, let me have a toast bread.
opts: How do you like to get for breakfast?
Is there any bread in your kitchen?
What sort of bread do you like?
Do you have to invite me to breakfast?
questions.exp: The response specifies a type of bread ('toast bread'), so the question must ask about preferences ('What sort of bread do you like?').

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Rosa: What do you think about his arrival?
Girum: __________________________.
opts: I think he’ll arrive on time
Arrivals are now a days uncertain
questions.exp: This is a grammatically complete and polite prediction matching the question.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Mesfin: Would you mind opening the door?
Elsa: _____________________________.
opts: Why don’t you open the door
questions.exp: To agree to a 'Would you mind' request, we respond with a negative ('No, not at all') to indicate we do not mind performing the task.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Caller: Hello, can I speak to Ato Geday, please?
Receiver: _____________________________.
opts: I’m afraid you have got the wrong number
Your number is wrong, please change
questions.exp: A standard, polite telephone expression when a caller reaches the wrong person is 'I'm afraid you have got the wrong number'.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Waiter: What will you have sir?
Guest: ________________________.
opts: It doesn’t matter. Don’t repeat it again.
What do you mean? Anything to drink.
May I have a menu, please?
questions.exp: A customer at a restaurant would ask to see the menu ('May I have a menu, please?') before ordering.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Customer: Do you have a changing room?
Shop assistant: _________________.
questions.exp: A shop assistant would politely guide a customer to the changing room by saying 'Certainly, it’s over there'.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Hany: Do you think it will rain today?
Petros: ______________________.
opts: I guess so, because the sky is cloudy.
I think so, the sun still shines.
I don’t think so, the clouds are so dark.
I’m sorry, but I can’t agree with you.
questions.exp: A cloudy sky is physical evidence supporting a prediction of rain ('I guess so, because the sky is cloudy').

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Solomon: What do you think about the film?
Ahimed: I think __________________.
questions.exp: This is the only grammatically complete and correct evaluation of the film.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Aster: I feel tired and dizzy.
Alem: I think ______________.
opts: I must take some rests too
you should lie down and have some rest
you can see the doctor tonight
questions.exp: The most appropriate response to someone feeling tired and dizzy is to offer supportive health advice ('you should lie down...').

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Sisay: Have you ever heard that Natan and Dagmawit are getting married?
Semegn: Oh, really? What a surprise. ______________________.
opts: I’m very pleased to hear that
Of course, I heard about it
questions.exp: Expressing happiness ('I'm very pleased to hear that') is a standard, polite reaction to marriage news.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Chris: Why didn’t you call me back?
Jordan: I was swamped all day.
Chris: Still, you could have______________________
opts: sent me a text at least.
questions.exp: The modal 'could have' is followed by a past participle. 'sent me a text at least' is grammatically correct and logically fits.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Belay: How can I finish all this in time?
Clerk: __________________________
opts: If I were you, I would ask friends to help
I know you are so much hard working
questions.exp: Giving advice using a second conditional structure ('If I were you, I would...') is appropriate here.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Goitom: This will get more expensive next week.
Lulit: __________________________
opts: Perhaps, it might remain the same
questions.exp: This offers a logical alternative prediction to Goitom's statement.

questions.q:
Select the most appropriate response to complete the dialogue:
Stranger: How can I get to the Ministry of Education?
Beza: __________________________________
opts: Sorry, I am a stranger myself.
Lots of people ask this question.
questions.exp: A standard, polite way to decline giving directions when you do not know the way is: 'Sorry, I am a stranger myself'.

questions.q:
Identify the genre of writing that the following extract is taken from:
"To make coffee first wash the coffee beans and roast them next, grind the roasted coffee beans with mortar and pestle and then add the coffee flour to the boiling water in a kettle."
questions.exp: This extract outlines a step-by-step process or recipe, which is characteristic of expository (informative/process) writing.

questions.q:
Identify the genre of writing that the following extract is taken from:
"I was hurrying across the campus to get to a class. As it had rained heavily all morning, I had to cross a path full of water. After a few minutes' walk, I called out to two friends..."
questions.exp: This extract describes a personal experience or story using first-person pronouns, which is characteristic of narrative writing.

questions.q:
Identify the genre of writing that the following extract is taken from:
"... However, it would be a serious mistake to replace libraries with tablets. First, digital books and resources are associated with less learning and more problems than print resources..."
questions.exp: This extract presents a claim and supports it with reasoning to convince the reader, which is characteristic of argumentative writing.

questions.q: Which of the following is an appropriate ending for a formal letter that begins with 'Dear Sir/Madam'?
questions.exp: When the recipient's name is unknown and the letter begins with 'Dear Sir/Madam', we sign off with 'Yours faithfully'.

questions.q: When writing a formal business letter, what element do you place at the top right-hand corner of the page?
questions.exp: Under standard block/semi-block business letter formats, the writer's address is placed at the top right-hand corner.
